text.content:
What this module does

Substances and Paraphernalia ships ready-to-use illicit substances (consumables) and the paraphernalia required to consume them. When a substance with required paraphernalia is used through dnd5e's Activity flow, the module checks whether the actor has the required gear in a ready state and either allows the use or shows a Missing paraphernalia dialog with a Use anyway override.

How gating works

Each substance carries a list of paraphernalia requirement groups in its module flags. The outer list is AND (every group must be satisfied); each group's anyOf list is OR (any single referenced paraphernalia satisfies that group). References can be either compendium UUIDs (stable, recommended for shipped content) or kebab-case slugs that match a paraphernaliaId flag on the actor's items (the author-friendly path).

Paraphernalia must be ready:

  • Equipment (e.g. a pipe) must be equipped.
  • Consumable paraphernalia (e.g. rolling papers) must have quantity > 0.
  • Attunement-required paraphernalia must be attuned on the actor's copy.

The dialog names the substance and the missing groups, with reasons annotated — “(not equipped)” or “(not attuned)” — when at least one candidate is on the actor but unready. Every user (GM or player) sees the same dialog with the same buttons; the player override is intentional and mirrors how dnd5e surfaces missing-resource overrides for spells, Lay on Hands, and the like. Abuse is a social problem the GM handles at the table.

Authoring your own substances and paraphernalia

The full flag schema lives in docs/flag-schema.md in the module distribution. The short version:

flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"] = {
  kind: "substance" | "paraphernalia",
  setting: "fantasy" | "sciFi" | "modern",
  category: "stimulant" | "mindAltering" | "performanceEnhancing", // substance
  paraphernaliaId: "kebab-case-slug",                              // paraphernalia
  requiredParaphernalia: [{ anyOf: ["slug-or-Compendium.UUID"] }], // substance
  schemaVersion: 1
};

For substances to consume and self-destruct correctly, the substance's Activity must have a Consumption target of type Item Uses with value 1, and the item's Uses must be configured with max: 1 and autoDestroy: true.

Limitations

The gate only fires for substances used through the dnd5e Activity flow (dnd5e.preUseActivity). RP-style direct inventory edits, custom macros that bypass Activities, or right-click → Use outside the Activity flow will bypass paraphernalia gating. This is intentional at v0.1; if a player consumes a substance through a back channel, the GM handles it at the table.

Optional integrations

The module knows about four optional Foundry modules. None are required.

  • Dynamic Active Effects (dae) — required for substances whose Active Effects use DAE-only modes. Those effects carry requiresDae: true; without DAE active, a warning is shown at use time. The world setting Block substances that require DAE when DAE is missing can promote the warning to a hard block.
  • Midi-QoL — automated saves and chained workflow effects. Deeper integration is post-MVP.
  • Times Up — reliable Active Effect expiration on durations.
  • Token Magic FX — visual filters tied to substance effects. Post-MVP.

To hide the ready-time notice listing missing-but-recommended modules, enable the client setting Suppress integration warnings.

Settings reference

  • Enforce paraphernalia requirements (world, default on) — master switch for gating.
  • Block substances that require DAE when DAE is missing (world, default off) — promotes DAE warning to a hard block.
  • Suppress integration warnings (client, default off) — hides the ready-time integration notice.
  • Debug logging (client, default off) — verbose console logging.

The included macro Toggle Paraphernalia Enforcement flips the master switch from the hotbar.

Troubleshooting

  • The dialog doesn't appear when I expect it. Confirm the substance has flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"].kind = "substance" and a non-empty requiredParaphernalia. Confirm Enforce paraphernalia requirements is on. Confirm the substance is being used via an Activity (the chat-card Use button on the item sheet), not a manual chat message.
  • Override fires but the substance still won't auto-destroy. Open the activity's Consumption tab and confirm there is an Item Uses target with value 1. Without it the activity runs but never increments uses, so autoDestroy never fires.
  • Effect appears applied but nothing seems to happen. The effect may use a DAE-only mode. Confirm DAE is active and that the effect carries requiresDae: true; if so, that's the warning the module is trying to surface.
